current_milestone: M4_Presentable_CLOSED (VERIFIED). P9 — Rendering, Audio & Visual Grammar is VERIFIED (docs/verification/P9.md). Next: P10 — Content Generation, already unlocked by M3, now the active phase in the DAG.
completed_since_last_snapshot: S9.6 COMPLETED (REQ-071 audio share, REQ-170 WebAudio share): render/audio/{AudioDevice,PatchCompiler,CueDerivation,AudioExecutor}.ts. AudioDevice is the enumerated AudioContext subset + createTraceAudioDevice. deriveAudioCues(previous, current, grammar) is pure/transition-driven, emitting ONLY landed/defeat/goal cues (dm-0099 — no jump or checkpoint cue, a documented scope narrowing from the original plan prose). compilePatch turns a category's SynthPatch (from PaperStylePack.audioPatch, S9.2) into a node-graph plan. createAudioExecutor exposes muted/scheduleCues/muteForBreak/resume; dm-0100: mute stops ALL device calls, not just new scheduling. 11 tests (Audio.test.ts).

S9.7 COMPLETED (REQ-132 P9 share, REQ-163 async-delivery share, REQ-002): render/assets/{AssetManifest,AssetFetcher,AssetLoader}.ts — the seventh versioned schema. loadCriticalTier blocks first render; streamDeferredTier streams the rest, degrading (skipping) individually on failure without blocking readiness. dm-0101 fixed a RenderIsolation.test.ts false positive: the blanket /\bfetch\s*\(/ pattern flagged AssetFetcher's legitimate `fetch(url): Promise<...>` interface declaration — narrowed to a smarter isForbiddenFetchCall() that excludes method calls/signature declarations. 12 tests (AssetManifest.test.ts + AssetLoader.test.ts).

S9.8 COMPLETED (REQ-170/171/001/002): render/shell/{GameShell,ViewportModel,InputCapture,PortalSdk,PortalLifecycle}.ts. GameShell.advanceFrame wraps Engine+StateManager+CANONICAL_PIPELINE — PROJECTION PURITY PROVEN: sim state bit-identical to a headless run given the same InputFrames (GameShell.test.ts), proven against a REAL spike-defeat-and-reload cycle (not a fabricated WorldState) for gameplayStart/Stop re-arming. ViewportModel is pure letterbox/DPR-cap[1,2]/fullscreen math. InputCapture is edge-triggered jump/reset key-state mapping. PortalLifecycle orchestrates Poki's init->gameLoadingFinished ordering and wraps commercialBreak with mute+input-suspend (try/finally, restores even on rejection). dm-0102 fixed a second RenderIsolation false positive (ViewportInput.devicePixelRatio field name vs. the real window.devicePixelRatio hazard, already caught by the separate window check — the standalone pattern was removed as redundant). dm-0103: render/platform/ (the real browser-global bindings — canvas, rAF, AudioContext, the PokiSDK CDN global) is DELIBERATELY NOT CREATED this phase — no build/bundle pipeline or browser test harness exists yet (that's P11's job); every seam here is proven only against hand-rolled fakes. 24 tests (GameShell/ViewportModel/InputCapture/PortalLifecycle .test.ts).

S9.9 COMPLETED (REQ-130/131 P9 render/UI share): render/tooling/{Overlay,EditorSurface,InspectorBindings}.ts — the ONLY render/ area importing tools/ (dm-0081). Overlay.paintOverlays composes all six P8 tools/debug/Overlay.ts descriptor kinds verbatim into semantic, device-free primitives (rect/polyline/label) — colour/stroke choices deliberately deferred to a future render/platform/ executor, same precedent as dm-0103 (dm-0104). EditorSurface.compileEditorScene paints a P8 LevelDraft through the REAL StylePack/Grammar pipeline for terrain+entities (WYSIWYG with the shipped game) by reusing render/scene/SceneCompiler.ts's terrain-autotile helpers — GENERALIZED this slice from WorldState-scoped to bare-TilemapDef-scoped (a pure signature narrowing, S9.3 is COMPLETED-not-VERIFIED so amendment was allowed) so EditorSurface reuses them with zero duplicated autotile logic (dm-0105); grid/selection are separate StylePack-free editor-only chrome. mapEditorInput maps raw pointer/key events to P8's OWN EditorCommand union / undo / redo, never re-implementing them (proven: placeEntity through this mapping still lands grid-snapped via P8's own applyCommand). InspectorBindings.applyInspectorCommand is a thin closed UI-command union dispatching to exactly one Inspector method call each, zero new mutation logic (dm-0106). Zero P8 module modified. 18 new tests (ToolingOverlay/EditorSurface/InspectorBindings .test.ts).

S9.10 COMPLETED (REQ-163, THE IRD P9 EXIT CONDITION): render/quality/{QualityProfile,QualityController,QualityTier}.ts. Zero-assumption finding at slice start (dm-0107): particles live entirely in ParticlePool (never enter DrawList); grain/decor are baked into PaperStylePack.rasterize()'s bitmap content — NEITHER is a separate DrawItem field, so the original planning prose's 'reduced-particles/no-deferred-decoration/no-grain' per-category dropping does not correspond to anything in the shipped code. The tier ladder instead gates via a generic nonCriticalBudget truncation over DrawList — applyTier keeps every critical:true item unconditionally (the REQ-016 x REQ-163 interlock, pinned as a DrawItem.critical contract comment since S9.3/S9.4) and truncates only critical:false items, order-preserving. QualityController.reportFrameTime is a pure frame-time EMA state machine (QualityControllerState is render-layer-only, threaded exactly like CameraState, never WorldState/GameState): degrade takes priority over upgrade, steps exactly one rung per pressured cooldown-elapsed tick; upgrade additionally requires stabilityWindowTicks CONSECUTIVE good ticks (any bad tick resets the streak to zero); both directions share one cooldownTicks floor, which is what actually prevents boundary oscillation (hysteresis alone is not sufficient at tick-by-tick cadence). 12 tests (QualityController.test.ts) proving the exact IRD exit condition: hysteresis gap, cooldown-blocks-oscillation, stability-window-must-be-consecutive, never-drops-critical (incl. budget 0), tier-index bounds, purity.

S9.11 COMPLETED (M4 CLOSURE): docs/verification/P9.md authored — slice/deliverable/proof map for all 11 slices, exit-gate evidence, per-REQ verdicts (dm-0008), a REAL subtractivePass() run over the 11 render/ subtrees (11 kept, 0 removal candidates, 0 incomplete, clean:true) plus an honest supplementary export-level scan noting render/tooling/ and render/quality/ are not yet wired into GameShell (not orphaned debt — render/quality/ genuinely needs render/platform/'s real timing, deferred to P11; render/tooling/ awaits whatever dev-tool UI shell hosts it, a P9/P10-adjacent integration not itself a P9 deliverable). docs/requirements_backlog.md: REQ-070/071/130/131/150 flipped VERIFIED; REQ-001/002/161/163/170/171 flipped NOT_STARTED->IN_PROGRESS (11 exact-string edits total, diff-verified, nothing else touched). docs/IRD.md: P9 VERIFIED section + Milestone M4 CLOSED. docs/task_slices.md: S9.11 row COMPLETED.
